name: Cocoa Raspberry Sweethearts
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Sugar, divided (1/2 cup for yolks, 1/4 cup for whites)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Grease a 13 x 9 inch cake pan and line with wax paper or parchment paper. Set aside.
3. Sift together flour, cocoa powder, and salt; set aside.
4. In a large bowl, beat egg whites until stiff peaks form. Gradually add 1/2 cup sugar, 1 tablespoon at a time, while continuing to beat until stiff peaks form.
5. In another large bowl, beat egg yolks with remaining sugar until very light and lemon-colored (about 6 minutes). Stir in water and vanilla extract.
6. Sift dry ingredients over the yolk mixture and whisk gently.
7. Gently fold the wet mixtures together.
8. Spread batter evenly in prepared pan.
9. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Remove from oven and let cool on a rack.
10. Invert onto a cutting board; peel off paper.
11. Using heart-shaped cookie cutters, carefully cut out hearts, minimizing gaps between them.
12. Sprinkle icing sugar over the hearts and arrange on serving plates.
13. Drizzle raspberry and chocolate sauces around the hearts and garnish with fresh raspberries and mint leaves.
Easy Raspberry Sauce:
1. Thaw frozen raspberries in light syrup; place in a blender.
2. Puree until smooth, then strain through a sieve to remove seeds.
3. Transfer puree to a saucepan, stir in 1 tablespoon cornstarch, and cook over medium heat until thickened and clear.
4. Cool completely, then refrigerate.
Chocolate Sauce:
1. In a medium saucepan, whisk together granulated sugar and cocoa powder.
2. Gradually add water while whisking constantly.
3. Cook over medium heat until boiling, stirring often.
4. Reduce heat and simmer gently for 4 minutes until slightly thickened.
5. Remove from heat and stir in vanilla extract.
6. Allow to cool completely before refrigerating up to several days.
